titleStmt.title: SII 38.494: original edition by S. Swaminathan
No. 494. (A.R. No. 494 of 1912.) ŪṬṬATTŪR, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT. On the south wall, first prākāra, Siddharatnēśvara temple.

p.hi: King: Rājādhirāja II Year: 5, Mēsha, śu. di 3 Monday, Tiruvōṇam = 24th June 1168 A.D. provided Mēsha śu. 3 for Mithuna, ba. 3.

summary.p: Built in and incomplete. Records that a brāhmaṇa lady Āḷavandāḷ Sāni, wife of a certain person (•••āyaṇan) of Tippaikuḍi in Pagaiyaṟutt-ulakāṇḍchōḷachaturvēdimaṅgalam in Veḷḷalaiyūr-kūṟṟam (a sub-division) of Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu, donated her land and residential sites (kāṇi), which she had inherited after the demise of her husband, to the god Tirutoṟṟattūr-uḍaiyār in Ūṟṟattūr-nāḍu.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
vattikaḷ śrī Irājādhirājadevaṟku yāṇṭu 5vatu meṣa nāyaṟṟu pūrvapakṣattu tritiyaiyum tiṅkaṭkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa tiruvoṇattiṉāḷ
ja vaḷaṉāṭṭu veḷḷalaiyūṟ kaṇṭattu caṅkeṉatiyāṉa pakaiyaṟuttulakāṇṭacoḻaccaturvveti maṅkalattu tippaikuṭi
�yaṇan brāhmaṇi Āḷavantāṭ cāṉiyenāna bharttāvin Abhāvattu Eṉṉutāy Iruntuḷḷa kāṇi Iṉṉāṭṭu Ūṟṟattūrṉāṭṭu tiruttoṟṟattūr Uṭaiyār �
nārkku dhanmatāṉamāka narRead nīrvārttu kuṭutta nilaṅkaḷu maṉaikaḷumāvaṉa Ivvūr teṉkaḻaṉiyil śrī tevivatikku meṟkukku
�ḻa vāykkālukku teṟku 1 kūli 5 catirattu 5mukkāṇi bama 2 kūli 5 catirattu rūpāy mūṉṟumātmaorumā kārai kaṟpakavāykkālukku teṟku 4 6—1 tuṇṭattu
nāṭaṟi pukaḻaṉ vāykkālukku teṟku 4 kūli 3 kūñairaṇṭumā � Œkiḻ mūṉṟumātmam kulottuṅkacoḻavāykkālukku vaṭakku 1 kūli 5 cati
vatikku kiḻakku kulottuṅkacoḻavāykkālu vaṭa catirattu » mūṉṟumā tma Itil » 1araikkāl m vaṭa ṉ vapraRead vipranārāyaṇavatikkuk kiḻakkum tu
kālukku vaṭakku 3 1000 2 catirattu » tmalam Ūrnattattukku teṟkil maṅkalavitiyil tenciṟakil kallaikkuṭaiyān kuṭiiruppu maṉaikkum
kai teruvil kuṟṟamaṟṟi kuṭi Iruppu maṉai 1m Itaṉ kiḻakku maṉain=ta mukkālum kāraikaṟpaka ceriyil kallakamuṭaiyān Uyyavaṉtāṉ Oḻukkaikku kiḻa
Digital edition of SII 38.494 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
